news.title: World Cup, Ballon d’Or, Champions League, Euros … has Rodri completed football? | The Knowledge
news.lede: Plus: the most World Cup assists, Ballon d’Or winners with fewest caps and what happens to World Cup balls? Mail us with your all of your questions and answers. “Adding the World Cup to his résumé means Rodri has now won the World Cup, the European Championship, the Champions League, and the Ballon d’Or,” writes Owen Bagnall. “Has anyone else ‘completed football’ like this?”

news.title: What the 2026 World Cup Finalists Reveal About History and Rivalry
news.lede: What the 2026 World Cup semifinalists reveal about rivalry, memory and the transformation of interstate competition Spain has already secured its place in the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final after defeating France. England and Argentina now meet in the second semifinal, with the winner advancing to face Spain on 19 July. The four semifinalists—Spain, France, England and Argentina—are connected not only by football, but by centuries of war, empire, revolution and changing balances of power. The comparison should not be taken too literally.

news.title: George Kittle, other NFL players push for grass after World Cup resurfacing
news.lede: The World Cup is finished, but NFL players hope grass fields are not. After football venues including MetLife Stadium in New Jersey swapped out synthetic turf for natural grass for the global soccer tournament, big-name players including George Kittle, Breece Hall and Jonathan Taylor urged the NFL to stick with grass. “Grass looks great at MetLife! Can we keep it that way @NFL??

news.title: World Cup becoming a 64-team tournament would create logistical challenges - so can Gianni Infantino's idea work?
news.lede: This year's World Cup is finally over - and after 48 teams took part, it was a long, long tournament. But just as we bid to say goodbye to the 2026 edition, this competition may be getting even bigger. FIFA president Gianni Infantino says any further expansion of the World Cup for 2030 - where six countries are set to host the tournament - ‌will be discussed now the 2026 World Cup has finished. And one solution FIFA are discussing is a 64-team tournament.

news.title: ‘Reclaim football’: Sepp Blatter joins La Liga chief in call for Gianni Infantino to resign
news.lede: Blatter attacks successor as Fifa president. Javier Tebas says system is ‘rotten at its core’. The Fifa president, Gianni Infantino, has come in for heavy criticism from his predecessor, Sepp Blatter, and the La Liga president, Javier Tebas, with the pair united in the belief that he should stand down. As Spain celebrated their World Cup win on Sunday, Tebas accused Infantino of “destroying the football industry” by prioritising the World Cup over national competitions.

news.title: Why Americans call it soccer while the rest of the world calls it football
news.lede: The World Cup Final is Sunday, with Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal set to face off for the trophy. Millions of Americans have been watching the sport all month — but most of them have been calling it something different than the rest of the world.So why do Americans say "soccer" while nearly everyone else says "football"? It all starts in 1848, when students at Cambridge University wrote the first set of rules for the game we know today. Those rules centered on kicking a ball with your foot — hence, football.

news.title: Argentina players turned their backs during Spain's FIFA World Cup celebrations; video goes viral - WATCH
news.lede: NEW DELHI: The FIFA World Cup 2026 final had everything - drama, controversy and ugly scenes. Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 to lift the FIFA World Cup 2026 title at the New York New Jersey Stadium, but the tension did not end with the final whistle. There were scuffles, heated arguments and even an apparent shove by the throat as tempers flared between players.The bad blood carried over into the trophy ceremony, where several Argentina players turned their backs while Spain celebrated with the World Cup trophy.

news.title: World Cup musings: Argentina let down Messi; England have regressed
